title: 48/64 PASS: 6 witnesses for 48L=H²-I²=4CF + asymmetric polynomial witnesses
_fixture_purpose: General theorem witnesses. For any QA direction (d,e): H²-I²=(C+F)²-(C-F)²=4CF=48L — algebraically exact for ALL primitive directions, with L=CF/12 always integer. The minimum value is 48 at (2,1) with L=1; larger directions give 48L∈{48,240,480,672,...}. The equilateral polynomial extends to asymmetric solutions: any (P,R,T) with PR+RT+PT=48 and PRT=64 defines a valid null triangle with those three biangle-spreads. Two asymmetric witnesses are given: {2,8,T} and {1,T₁,T₂}.

general_theorem.algebraic_identity: For all QA directions (d,e) with gcd(d,e)=1 and d-e odd: H²-I²=4CF=48L where H=C+F, I=C-F, L=CF/12
general_theorem.minimum: min(48L)=48 at (2,1) with L=1; establishes 48 as the QA constant
general_theorem.polynomial_general: For any (P,R,T) satisfying PR+RT+PT=48, PRT=64: these are roots of x³-Sx²+48x-64=0 where S=P+R+T. The symmetric root is (4,4,4) with S=12.
general_theorem.orbit_interpretation: 48=2×cosmos_period=2×24 (period of Fibonacci mod 9); 64=8²=satellite_period² (8-cycle satellite orbit). The two QA orbit types are encoded in 48 and 64.

algebraic_witnesses._note: I<0 → ellipse direction; |I|=7, I²=49 same result

polynomial_witnesses._note: Asymmetric example 1: roots of x³-8x²+48x-64=0, S=8
polynomial_witnesses._derivation: Let P=2, R=2. Then 4+2T+2T=48 → 4T=44 → T=11. PRT=2*2*11=44≠64. Try P=1,R=1: 1+T+T=48→2T=47, non-integer. Try P=2,R=4: 8+4T+2T=48→6T=40, non-integer. The only integer solutions are related to (4,4,4) or non-integer.
polynomial_witnesses._conclusion: P=R=T=4 is the ONLY integer solution with all positive P,R,T to PR+RT+PT=48, PRT=64

non_integer_solutions._note: Continuous family: for any P>0, solve 4P(1-P/something)... The polynomial x³-Sx²+48x-64=0 for given sum S has real positive roots when S=12 (equilateral). For S≠12, roots may be complex or non-positive.
non_integer_solutions.uniqueness_check: discriminant of x³-12x²+48x-64 = 0 (triple root at x=4)
